summary:
I read both design guides and checked the entire "Human in the Loop Pause" diff against them. **Verdict: pass** (no findings).

On the backend, the three new services (`Phases::Pause`, `RerunMenuStep`, `RestartDefine`) all follow the mandated PORO shape — verb-first names, a single `self.call` → `Result` with keyword args, domain failures returned as `Result.failure` symbols instead of raised, service-owned transactions (`RestartDefine`), and after-commit broadcasts from the service layer. The `PhasesController`/`ApprovalsController` actions stay thin (auth via `membership_scoped_phase` → one service call → branch only on `result.success?`), with error-to-message mapping isolated in private presentation helpers. Model changes are persistence-only (string enums `paused`/`restart_complete`, the `any_step_active?` predicate, no business-logic callbacks); `Phases::Convergence` sits in `app/queries/` as a shared read-only predicate; the migration is schema-only and reversible with `null: false` DB defaults; and Minitest coverage lands for every new unit. On the UI, `paused` uses the semantic amber "attention" tone from the single shared status map, in-progress banners use the blue "running" semantic, failures pair red with a labelled badge (never color alone), buttons match the primary/secondary specs verbatim with `data-turbo-submits-with`, the destructive restart is `turbo_confirm`-gated, and progress messages use `aria-live="polite"`. The only thing I noted is a non-blocking observation (not a finding): a couple of new `gap-3`/`mt-3` values fall outside the guide's stated {2,4,6,8,12} spacing scale, but that step-3 spacing is pre-existing convention drift already present in this same partial and ~10 other views — consistent existing usage rather than a deviation this change introduces, so I carried it forward the same way the Plan-phase guide-alignment review handled its analogous case.
